records.ety: [L. dissociatus, p. p. of dissociare to dissociate; dis- + sociare to unite, associate, socius companion. See Social.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dissociated; p. pr. & vb. n. Dissociating.]

defs: To separate from fellowship or union; to disunite; to disjoin; as, to dissociate the particles of a concrete substance.
quotes: Before Wyclif's death in 1384, John of Gaunt had openly dissociated himself from the reformer.
